_source: Synthetic representative profile for Singh 2014 PNAS (PMID 25313065) sulforaphane RCT. Trial inclusion was male age 13-27 with moderate-to-severe ASD; NOT biomarker-stratified. Sulforaphane's mechanism is Nrf2 / glutathione / oxidative-stress modulation, so the cohort entry targets PHE-0002 (mitochondrial dysfunction). This representative encodes the population-typical mild oxidative-stress signal — the same framing as Hardan 2012 NAC (rrc_004) since both trials are unstratified and target the same biological axis. The trial's responder rate (9/26 = 0.346, both ABC+SRS ≥30%) should be predicted by a near-baseline PHE-0002 loading on this profile.

organic_acids_urine._mild_oxidative_stress_signal_consistent: Singh 2014 was unstratified; encoded as the population-typical mild signal that sulforaphane's Nrf2/glutathione mechanism would address

_engine_expectation.rationale: Mild lactate elevation drives PHE-0002 loading via biomarker shift (+0.30). Singh 2014 was unstratified, so this comparison tests engine baseline + mild-signal calibration vs the trial's 34.6% responder rate. Same architectural test as Hardan 2012 NAC (rrc_004); should give a similar near-baseline PHE-0002 loading. Both trials target the same oxidative-stress axis with population-typical (unstratified) ASD cohorts.
